# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default

- name: cloud-sql
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    settings:
      tier: db-n1-standard-1
      dataDiskType: PD_SSD
      dataDiskSizeGb: 10
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: true

- name: cloud-storage
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: us-central1
    storageClass: REGIONAL

- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.urlMap
  properties:
    defaultService: $(ref.backend-service.selfLink)
  resources:
  - name: backend-service
    type: compute.v1.backendService
    properties:
      backends:
      - group: $(ref.instance-group.selfLink)
      healthChecks:
      - $(ref.http-health-check.selfLink)
    resources:
    - name: instance-group
      type: compute.v1.instanceGroup
      properties:
        zone: us-central1-a
        namedPorts:
        - name: http
          port: 80
    - name: http-health-check
      type: compute.v1.httpHealthCheck
      properties:
        port: 80
        requestPath: /health-check
        checkIntervalSec: 5
        timeoutSec: 5
        unhealthyThreshold: 2
        healthyThreshold: 2
